skill.name: web-research-assistant
skill.description: AI-powered web research assistant that uses the BrowserAct API to search the internet and generate structured research reports when direct web access is restricted.
skill.summary: Search the web and generate structured research reports using the BrowserAct API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: After AI evaluation, all 43 static analysis findings are determined to be false positives. The skill is a legitimate web research assistant that uses the BrowserAct API for web searches. The __import__('datetime') call at research.py:125 uses an unusual lazy import pattern but only imports a standard library module with a hardcoded string. All network calls, filesystem writes, and credential access patterns are consistent with the skill's documented purpose. SKILL.md backtick findings are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. No evidence of prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. Risk level downgraded from static analyzer's suggested NEEDS_AI/SKIP to LOW.

low_findings.title: Unconventional lazy import using __import__
low_findings.description: Uses __import__('datetime') at runtime instead of a standard import statement. While functionally equivalent to importing the standard datetime module, this pattern is unusual and could be mistaken for dynamic code execution by automated scanners.

content.user_title: Research the Web with BrowserAct API
content.value_statement: This skill solves the problem of restricted web access during AI-assisted research. It searches the internet using the BrowserAct API and generates structured research reports with source citations.
actual_capabilities: Searches the web using configurable search engines including Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo
Filters search results by content type and time range for targeted research
Extracts key facts, statistics, and source citations from search results
Generates formatted research reports in markdown or JSON format
Saves research reports to user-specified file paths
Retrieves current information when primary web access is restricted
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key and MCP token to function
Search quality depends on the underlying search engine and API availability
Cannot access content behind paywalls or login-required pages
Limited to 50 results per query and text-based content only

use_cases.title: Market Research for Business Analysts
use_cases.description: A business analyst researches competitor products and market trends by running targeted web searches with time and content filters.

use_cases.title: Academic Research for Students
use_cases.description: A student gathers information on a research topic from multiple sources, with automatic citation extraction and report generation.

use_cases.title: Technical Research for Developers
use_cases.description: A developer searches for technical documentation, API references, and troubleshooting information when direct web access is blocked.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for the latest information about [topic]. Provide a summary of the key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wants to research a simple topic

prompt_templates.title: Time-Limited News Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for news articles about [topic] published in the past week. Focus on [specific aspect] and list the top 5 sources with publication dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs current news on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [company name] and its top 3 competitors. Use DuckDuckGo as the search engine and retrieve up to 15 results per company. Compare their key features, pricing models, and market positioning.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Research Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Conduct a thorough web search on [research topic]. Use all available search engines, filter for articles and reports only, and gather results from the past year. Format the output as a detailed markdown report with executive summary, key findings, statistics, and source citations. Save the report to research_report.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user generating a complete research deliverable

output_examples.input: What are the latest trends in renewable energy?
output_examples.output: A markdown report with an executive summary noting growth in solar and wind energy, key statistics on energy adoption rates, citations from 10 sources including industry reports and news articles, and recommendations for further research.
A structured summary listing the top 8 search results with titles, URLs, and snippets, key facts about new features in Python 3.13, and a list of official documentation sources.

best_practices: Use specific and targeted search queries to get more relevant results
Apply time range filters when you need current or recent information
Cross-reference key findings across multiple sources for accuracy
anti_patterns: Using vague one-word queries that return too many unrelated results
Relying on a single search result as the sole source for critical information
Running searches without setting appropriate content type or time filters

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct API key and MCP token. Set them as BROWSERACT_API_KEY and BROWSERACT_MCP_TOKEN environment variables.

faq.question: What search engines does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Google, Bing, and DuckDuckGo as configurable search engines.

faq.question: How many search results can I get per query?
faq.answer: You can request up to 50 results per query. The default is 10 results.

faq.question: Can this skill access paywalled content?
faq.answer: No, it can only access publicly available content through search engine results.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill can generate reports in markdown format or raw JSON format.

faq.question: Is my API key safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the API key is read from your environment variables and sent only to the BrowserAct API server for authentication.
